sourceMovie.title: Star Wars: The Last Jedi
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Star Wars: The Last Jedi works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Star Wars: The Last Jedi clicked for you, Guardians of the Galaxy (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Star Wars: The Last Jedi clicked for you, Star Wars (1977) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Star Wars: The Last Jedi?
faq.answer: Try Guardians of the Galaxy, Star Wars, Star Wars: The Force Awakens—each shares DNA with Star Wars: The Last Jedi while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Star Wars: The Last Jedi?
faq.answer: Star Wars: The Last Jedi (2017) is best described as Adventure, Action, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Star Wars: The Last Jedi part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Star Wars Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
